description: Are you looking for a patient-focused, innovation-driven company to inspire you and empower you to shine? Join us as a Senior Manager, Feasibility based in Łódź, Poland reporting to the Director, Feasibility. At Takeda, we are transforming the pharmaceutical industry through our R&D-driven market leadership and being a values-led company. To do this, we empower our people to work towards their potential through life-changing work. Certified as a Global Top Employer, we offer stimulating careers, and work toward excellence in everything we do. We foster an inclusive, collaborative workplace, in which our global teams are united by an unwavering commitment to provide Better Health and a Brighter Future to people around the world. Here, you will be a necessary contributor to our inspiring, bold mission. GOALS: Conduct data-driven feasibility assessments for Phase I – IV clinical studies, including protocol design optimization, country/site feasibility and selection, enrollment strategy, and modeling. Use all competitive intelligence data to develop data-driven study start-up forecasts, benchmarking assumptions, and accurate enrollment plans. Contribute to implementing creative processes, methodologies, data and technologies to ensure ongoing delivery and continuing improvement of Feasibility and Enrollment Forecasting Services. Participate in and/or lead global projects representing CSS&E and GDO to support Takeda R&D goals. ACCOUNTABILITIES: Develop feasibility analyses to maximize efficiency, effectiveness, and acceleration in Takeda's operational execution of its clinical research studies. Ensure standards are applied to the feasibility process across project portfolios and direct continuous improvement activities while developing TA-aligned strategies. Collect and analyze all data (e.g. local treatment standard of care, available treatment options approved/reimbursed, local incidence/prevalence of disease, access to targeted patient population, drug landscape, historical/current clinical trial landscape) to influence and inform protocol development, operational plans, and establish a geographic country footprint and proposed sites for participation in a clinical study. Leverage leading industry tools and data sources to provide data on startup timelines, past recruitment rates and performance (data quality, startup cycle time, query rate, protocol deviations, inspection results) to assist project teams with study initiation timeline forecasting. In partnership with internal stakeholders- combine and integrate data to provide clear feasibility recommendations to strengthen data-driven trend analysis, identify root causes, and provide relevant recommendations across study teams and programs to accelerate study execution. Monitor actual patient enrollment performance of assigned studies. In collaboration with the Takeda study team and CRO, responsible for re-forecasting patient enrollment models to adjust for new influence factors (e.g. change in drug landscape, protocol amendment, increased screen failure rate). Determine risk/ mitigation planning. Maintain current therapeutic area knowledge and assess impact of evolving information on operational strategies. Exhibit the ability to perform root cause analysis and determine mitigation steps to removing roadblocks related to study execution and patient enrollment. Ability to provide data-driven recommendations to assist with decision making at a study level as well as provide program level support to help define asset strategy. Through strong analytical skills, develop deep insights, uncover unmet needs, collaborate within a global matrixed team. Act as a role model for Takeda’s values. ​ EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE: BS degree or international equivalent required; in life science (preferred). At least 4 years of direct responsibility in leading strategic feasibility assessments or equivalent data analytics role(s) in a healthcare or clinical research setting at a sponsor or CRO. 6+

We need your help to make this happen. Join our Innovation Capability Centre (ICC) in Bratislava, Slovakia. At Takeda’s ICC we Unite in Diversity Takeda is committed to creating an inclusive and collaborative workplace, where individuals are recognized for their backgrounds and the abilities they bring to our company. We are continuously improving our collaborators’ journey in Takeda, and we welcome applications from all qualified candidates. Here, you will feel welcomed, respected, and valued as an important contributor to our diverse team. The Opportunity As a Senior Delivery Manager you will provide Project and Portfolio Management leadership across Global IT, including value realization, communication, tracking, and visualization and leading excellence in delivery of those objectives. You will be responsible for implementing governance alignment and management, including sourcing, budgeting, compliance, project and portfolio Global IT processes. You will act as a key contributor to transforming Global IT units to the Product Centric operating model, by influencing and implementing change management, and providing guidance for transition from waterfall to agile methodologies. Responsibilities Identify and solve complex problems in Project/Program Management across Waterfall and Agile methodologies for implementation and/or administration of project governance requirements, feedback for process improvement, lessons learned, and program enhancements. Act as a point of escalation moderately complex, non-routine problems. Provides leadership to cross-functional teams and/or program composed of multiple products/work streams with medium complexity in the successful delivery of high-impact IT initiatives. Manages solutions to achieve identified objectives, drives program achievement, and influences decision-making on projects and focusing on business outcomes. Identify opportunities and business needs by ensuring alignment of project and product investments and initiatives with business strategy based on changing functional needs, resource capacity constraints, risk exposure, and interdependencies. Lead the implementation of strategic and core projects and programs, ensuring routine monitoring, measurement, and reporting of performance against budget, schedule, and business objectives. Identify barriers that may be slowing down projects and propose innovative solutions to remove inefficiencies and drive progress toward program goals. Provide periodic updates to senior management and monitor the execution of delivery team activities. Actively manage project governance and portfolio management processes to ensure that approved initiatives align with budget and resource constraints. Maintain visibility into project performance, as well as associated risks and issues, and ensure effective governance coordination and support across the project and program portfolio. Partner with Global Chapter and Service Line Leads to ensure compliance with, and appropriate use of, enterprise portfolio and project management tools. Contribute to key initiatives, projects, and organizational changes in collaboration with business stakeholders, Global IT Sponsors, and Global IT Partners. Support the development of a highly productive and effective delivery team through facilitation and coordination, ensuring excellence in the delivery of business outcomes. description: Job Posting Description Are you looking for a patient-focused company that will inspire you and support your career? If so, be empowered to take charge of your future at Takeda. Join us as a Specialty Business Manager for our Omaha, NE territory . Here, everyone matters, and you will be an important contributor to our inspiring, bold mission. As a Specialty Business Manager working on the Gastroenterology Specialty Sales Team, you will be empowered to drive product demand in targeted physician offices, key clinics and hospital accounts. How you will contribute: Research prospective Health Care Professionals (HCPs) to identify the right customer stakeholders to present complex clinical and business information on products and services including Gastroenterologists HCPs and professional or patient groups. Use insight and consultative selling techniques to teach HCPs about their industry and offer unique perspectives on their business, which link back to Takeda's solutions. Engage in clinical discussions with HCPs to discuss patients' needs, execute brand strategies and maximize sales growth within a specific geography. Coach customer stakeholders and build consensus for Takeda's solutions within their organization. Collaborate with partners on routing and resource utilization to maximize overall footprint performance. Independently and collaboratively strategizing for solving deal-level challenges. Attend all company-sponsored sales and medical meetings as directed by company management. Actively pursue learning and professional development on efficient sales, communication and product knowledge training. Execution of marketing strategies at the local level Demonstrated business and strategic planning skills to identify unique selling opportunities and adaptability to changing market conditions Demonstrated understanding of managed care landscape and how it influences/impacts business Strong collaboration skills and success working in teams Experience with injectable/infused IBD (Inflammatory Bowel Disorder) products Experience with managing and communicating complex reimbursement issues Biological product launch experience Experience in calling on Gastroenterologists LICENSES/CERTIFICATIONS: Valid Driver's License TRAVEL REQUIREMENTS: Ability to drive and/or fly to meetings and client sites Some overnight travel required – 25-50%, depending on geographic assignment TRAINING REQUIREMENTS: This position and continued employment is contingent upon the employee successfully passing mandatory product training which includes written and oral examinations. title: Career Moves: Caroline Slomski
description: is to attract top-tier talent from across the globe and a wide range of backgrounds while simultaneously strengthening Takeda's employer brand. To achieve our project goals, we place a strong emphasis on creating an inclusive work culture that fosters innovation and collaboration. Each team member is united in our purpose and brings their unique and complementary viewpoints to the work we do so that we can be successful. How do you ensure your team aligns with Takeda's goals? Aligning teams with Takeda's goals involves clear communication, setting strategic objectives and regular check-ins. We are a large, matrixed organization so collaboration and building awareness are incredibly important to our overall success. Takeda’s values are brought to life through something we refer to as Patient-Trust-Reputation-Business ‘P-T-R-B'. We put the patient at the center of everything we do. If it’s good for the patient, we ask ourselves if our actions are helping to build trust with society and enhance Takeda’s reputation. Provided we can speak affirmatively about the first three, we know good business results will follow. No matter the department, we all follow P-T-R-B; it unites us, fosters a sense of purpose in our work and affirms to us that the work we are undertaking is worthwhile and necessary. Describe a strategic decision you made that influenced a project's direction. For me, it’s less about decisions and more about an approach. We all have opinions on what we believe is the ‘best path forward,’ but those opinions are often bespoke and not reflective of a situation or a particular working environment. By leading with qualitative and quantitative data, the ‘best path' often reveals itself, and it’s not always what I would have pursued had I been asked to make a decision independent of information. From starting as an aspiring Kaizen facilitator, I progressed to become a proficient Lean and Six Sigma coach. This journey has not only expanded my professional capabilities but has also enriched my perspective on driving impactful change within an organization. How do you show resilience and adaptability in Takeda's dynamic environment? Resilience and adaptability are essential traits for navigating our environment. I believe in actively seeking , embracing, participating in and even leading change. With every change comes an opportunity for learning and growth, something I've consistently embraced in every role. Operating in a dynamic environment challenges us both personally and professionally. I view every instance of change, no matter its scale, as a chance to enhance agility, persistence, creativity and problem-solving skills. Working across various organizational levels has taught me not just flexibility but adaptability in how I communicate, ensuring my message resonates effectively with different audiences. I strongly advocate for nurturing relationships founded on trust and respect, as they provide crucial support and enable seamless collaboration during periods of change. My advice to others is to approach change with a positive mindset, continually reflect on and improve their responses, actively seek feedback and strive to thrive in evolving circumstances. Share an example of moving between functions at Takeda. What skills did you gain or transfer? After more than a decade in Quality and Regulatory Compliance, I made a shift into Business Excellence, leveraging my vast background to bring a unique perspective to continuous improvement initiatives. title: Career Moves: Filippo Ramondelli
description: . I am excited to continue my career here and to be a part of the innovative and transformative work that we are known for. Can you share how external experiences enhanced your skills, providing a significant boost to your career at Takeda? Through my experience outside, I was able to see multiple aspects of the pharmaceutical industry, from contract development and manufacturing organization (CDMO) and client management for external manufacturing to analytical and pharmaceutical development for innovative and biosimilar medicines to the CMC Lead experience which gave me the global attitude that I have also brought to my current role. This broadened perspective has allowed me to bring valuable insights and knowledge to my work, enabling me to approach challenges with a more holistic understanding of the pharmaceutical industry. Additionally, the exposure to different working environments and cultures has enhanced my adaptability and communication and interpersonal skills, which are essential in a global company. I bring several years of technical knowledge and cross-functional negotiation and project management. Overall, my external experiences have significantly contributed to my career growth and success. What about Takeda’s culture makes you feel like you belong? The focus and priority are given to a simple set of values and pillars to which I feel totally aligned. The emphasis on patient-centricity, integrity, and fairness resonates with me deeply, and I am proud to be part of a company that upholds such principles. Additionally, the diverse and inclusive environment makes me feel valued and respected, further solidifying my connection to the company's culture. I see these values lived by many colleagues around me every day. The attention to employees' growth and development is not a given in the current work environment but the company is really pushing to get everyone in the driving seat of their own development. title: Careers at Takeda
description: behind it is no less direct. “We see the patients we help, and even in tough moments, that gives us the energy to go the extra mile.” Where Susan describes innovation as something built across teams, Gert sees its impact at the end of that process, in the medicines delivered to patients. For him, that connection is also personal. “When you know that what you are doing can help people, that gives your work real purpose.” Different roles. Same direction. Everything we do leads back to patients. Why Build a Career at Takeda Zoom out, and a pattern emerges. People come to Takeda from different starting points. Clinical care. Technology. Manufacturing. Early career roles. What connects them is not the path they start on, but how that path evolves. Careers here move across functions, geographies and stages of life. They deepen, expand and, at times, take entirely new directions. What makes that possible is not just opportunity, but foundation. Our heritage creates the trust, credibility and stability that allow innovation to happen at scale. That same foundation gives us the confidence to grow, change direction and build careers that last. For those exploring careers at Takeda, that may be the difference: A place where you don’t have to choose between stability and ambition, because one makes the other possible. Explore where your path could lead at Takeda. As a global pharmaceutical company, Takeda has evolved across generations, expanding its reach, advancing science and adapting to new challenges. That continuity creates a rare balance. The company’s legacy builds trust and credibility, while its culture encourages people to take on new ideas, new roles and new ways of working. For employees, that combination does something important. It allows careers to grow over time, without losing their foundation. From Patient Care to Global Innovation For Cynthia Guo, Head of China Oncology Medical, that shift happened at a defining moment. Working as an oncologist, she saw firsthand how limited treatment options shaped patient outcomes, with led her to a career with Takeda Oncology . “I realized that while I could treat individual patients as a doctor, the real impact would come from making more treatment options available…this could benefit thousands of patients and save lives.” That realization didn’t pull her away from patient care. It expanded it. At Takeda, that expansion is made possible

Founded in 2001, Envision Pharma Group is a leading global technology-enabled strategic solutions partner for the life sciences industry, working with over 400 pharma and biotech organizations, including the top 20 pharmaceutical companies. At Envision Pharma Group, every role has purpose. Whether you work in scientific and client-facing teams, or in functions such as Finance, HR, and IT, your contribution helps deliver better outcomes for patients and partners worldwide. We are a global, collaborative community of thinkers, builders, scientists, strategists, and enablers, united by a culture that empowers innovation, champions excellence, and values every contribution. If you’re talented, driven, and motivated, we’d love to hear from you. Explore career opportunities EPG TOGETHER We are a diverse group of individuals with unique skills, talents, and experiences who thrive when we collaborate. EXCELLENCE We strive to excel for our clients and each other – never settling – because mediocrity is never an option PEOPLE We work to help each other thrive by supporting, respecting, and inspiring GROWTH We champion bold thinking and embrace transformation to create an environment where innovation flourishes Grow. Learn. Thrive. At Envision, development is continuous, connecting learning, performance, and career growth. From day one, you’ll be supported to build new skills, expand your impact, and unlock your potential. Our onboarding experience provides a strong foundation, while ongoing learning, clear goals, and regular feedback help you grow with confidence and perform at your best. We combine digital learning, practical experience, and collaboration across teams to strengthen capability and prepare you for what’s next. Open career conversations and meaningful development opportunities ensure you can progress, broaden your expertise, and shape your future. At Envision, you’re empowered to perform, realise your potential, and build a career that grows with you. Belongi

description: Your working environment, colleagues and opportunities for personal and professional development are of paramount importance when it comes to choosing your employer. We are proud to have achieved a 3-star accreditation with Best Companies ™ for a fifth consecutive year. These accreditations were awarded based on input from our team on a wide range of topics such as our work environment, the company’s values and management. In 2019, we were presented with the Queen’s Award for International Trade, recognising our successful international expansion. In an episode of the “ The Pioneers ” podcast our CEO, Sophie Costello, explores with the host our company culture and values. We have also been invited to contribute at multiple Best Companies Live events, including two panel sessions that exemplified two of our key organisational strengths. In the first, Sophie Costello spoke about leading with purpose . At the second event, our Head of Talent Strategy, Saoirse Leonard, discussed wellbeing and personal growth .

title: FAQs
description: . It also provides you with a true flavour of what the role entails. The first step is to submit a CV/resume and cover letter via the online application form found within each job advert . Your application will first be assessed by a member of the Talent Acquisition team. The proceeding stages of the recruitment process are dependent on which role you have applied for and full details will be provided by the Talent Acquisition team to ensure you are fully informed about each stage of the process. When applying for our Technical, Creative and Operations roles, you may be invited to complete a 15-minute telephone interview with a member of the Talent Acquisition team. During this call, you will explore your motivations for applying and the skills you can bring to the role. For most roles, you will be sent an assessment exercise to complete at home. The assessment will be tailored to the role you have applied for to assess the necessary knowledge, understanding and skills. Successful candidates will then be invited to an interview, either in our office or remotely, which will involve competency-based questions. For our Scientific and Technical roles, you may be asked to give a short presentation ahead of your interview, which you can prepare for in advance. This will last for 10 minutes and will be presented to two members of the recruitment team. The final stage of the recruitment process may involve a proofreading exercise which will last 45 minutes and assesses your attention to detail. 3. Who should I contact if I need reasonable adjustments in the selection process? As an employer we are committed to promoting and protecting the physical and mental health and wellbeing of all our colleagues and to ensure a fair and equitable recruitment process. Please email recruitment@costellomedical.com if you require any reasonable adjustments to the selection process, including the assessment or interview. We prefer that any information about health or disability is disclosed
qualifications: of the role you have applied for. We recommend that they are no longer than one page. 6. I can’t decide between a permanent role or an internship role; which one should I apply for? Most of our permanent roles, and all of our internship roles, are entry-level and suitable for recent graduates or those joining from other industries. No prior experience is needed for either role. If you are looking for a permanent opportunity, we recommend that you apply for a permanent role, for example the Analyst/Medical Writer role. However, if you are unsure if this is a field you wish to commit to in the longer term, and are therefore looking to gain experience, you may wish to consider an internship instead, for example the Analyst/Medical Writer Internship role. Our internship programmes provide a first-hand insight into life at a fast-growing company. Therefore, there are times when the responsibilities and typical workload of both permanent and internship team members overlap. 7. Am I allowed to submit multiple applications at one time? Due to the differing recruitment processes associated with each role, we can only consider one application from you at a time. Therefore, please decide which opportunity you would like to be considered for before submitting your application. Occasionally our Talent Acquisition team will suggest a different role would be more suitable for you than the one you have applied for. The details for each role, as well as the online application forms, can be found on our career pages with further information about a typical day for our colleagues available here . 8. Do you provide placement opportunities or work experience? title: Business Development Manager
description: Business Development Manager - Antibody Licensing Salary: £55000 - £60000 per annum REF Number: v-185124 Consultant: Naynesh Mistry Contact: 01246 457703 Date Published: 30.04.2026 Industry: Biotech Location: Remote work, England Functional Expertise: Clinical Operations/Development, Board & Leadership Job Title: Business Development Manager – Antibody Licensing Location: UK (Remote/Hybrid) Company: US-based Biotech (via Talentmark) Talentmark is partnering with a fast-growing, US-headquartered biotech at the forefront of antibody discovery and development. With cutting-edge platforms and a strong global footprint, the business is advancing a diverse pipeline across immuno-oncology, autoimmune and inflammatory diseases, neurology, and metabolic disorders. They are now looking for a scientifically strong individual to transition into a Business Development role focused on the UK market, supporting antibody licensing and strategic partnerships. This role offers a clear entry point into BD, working closely with global teams to assess opportunities, support deal activity, and help shape collaborations—ideal for someone with an antibody discovery background and a strong interest in moving into a commercial, partner-facing position. The Role As a Business Development Manager, you will support and progressively take ownership of identifying, evaluating, and executing strategic partnering opportunities across the UK. You’ll gain exposure to the full deal lifecycle while working cross-functionally with global stakeholders. Key Responsibilities: Identifying and supporting external opportunities for out-licensing, co-development, and strategic collaborations, with a focus on the UK market. Assisting in the development and presentation of business cases, including opportunity analysis and risk assessment. Supporting BD activities across outreach, proposal development, negotiations, and deal execution. Coordinating cross-functional due diligence with global teams across R&D, CMC, Clinical, Regulatory, and Finance. Contributing to alliance management activities post-deal. Supporting the development of global business development and licensing strategy. Representing the organisation at local and international industry, investor, and partnering conferences. Your Background PhD in Immunology, Pharmacology, Cancer Biology, or a related life sciences discipline. Strong background in antibody drug discovery, including target validation and hit-to-lead optimisation. Ability to interpret complex biological datasets (e.g. affinity, specificity, in vivo efficacy) and translate into clear therapeutic and licensing narrative (relevant insights). Knowledge of antibody modalities such as monoclonal antibodies (mAbs), bispecifics, and ADCs. Highly motivated, proactive, and commercially curious, with the drive and enthusiasm to build a career in business development.
